reviews.review_msg: the course is very simple and easy to learn yourself but as others said just focus on the tutorials. make sure attendance is good, write 5 short reflections, finish your final and midterm revisions (easy done in 10 mins) and you pretty much guarantee a free 50% just off that. course wise however the assignment details were not clear at all sadly.

Lectures were a waste of time, the entire course will be self taught. Professor Lin's slides were wordy, often taken from other sources, and questions were usually redirected to students. TAs were the main help and were great.
She was unorganized, unhelpful, and was not familiar with her own content. I highly discourage taking a course with her.

Professor Lin is disorganized, does not speak clearly, and her slides contain excessive text. Assignment instructions are unclear, and she once cancelled class at the last minute after I had already driven 30 minutes to campus. However, the tutorials were very helpful and clearly explained the lecture material.

Prioritize tutorials over lectures. There were no exams. The course was graded on tut participation, weekly reflections, a midterm, and a final paper (including revisions). Avoid AI use on papers (zero tolerance). Be aware that paper instructions were sometimes vague and the final had no rubric, but the TA support was great for clarification.

Lectures were super boring but the material is easy to teach yourself. The TA goes over the material anyway in the tutorial, but you have to read lots to prepare. There's almost nothing you're marked on, just two papers, and some short open-ended discussion post-style assignments.

i didn't go to lectures but she made the class really easy, no final or midterm, just 2 papers. there is a lot of reading to do to write your paper though. make sure to ask TAs for advice because there was a requirement for the paper she didn't include in the rubric so that made everyone lose marks for the first paper.
Prof and TA's were great! Although I struggle with my writing, it wasn't to hard to do well because of revisions. Half of the grade is basically for applying feedback and participating.

She was a good professor and I think our average is going be high (B+/A-). She gave students a chance to resubmit their work if they used AI and offered office hours to review mid-term paper submissions. She also let students defend their work if it got accused of being plagiarized or AI generated.

a very very easy class, no need to attend lectures or even do readings. Just have to show up to tutorial to get your attendance grades. Worst thing is she is awful at creating rubrics. Very misleading and not much sense, easy to get confused and lose sufficient marks. Make sure to ask your TA about papers as much as you can before writing.

I enjoyed the content of the course overall. Attendance was based on tutorials but you only had to attend 7 to get full credit. She does revisions for both papers (10%) and even allowed students who used AI for their papers to rewrite them properly and submit later with no points docked if they chose to (ie. not after getting caught)

The professor is kind, knowledgeable, but boring. I feel like she just copied and pasted from her slides. There is no point in actually attending the lecture. Her grades are based on tutorials, weekly discussions, and MOSTLY the 2 papers + revisions; however, her grading is confusing, and there's barely any criteria to follow.

lectures are definitely long, and lots of people leave half way. Very heavy reading but at least there is no textbook.  Make sure to go to tutorials and be prepared. The TAs were good for me and had open office hours, so def go. Graded by two papers, weekly reflections and participation.

Phil 100w. So far, the course is alright. The lectures are confusing, and many students leave halfway through. The paper guidelines are not very clear, nor is her teaching method in lectures. However, she teaches with passion and honestly cares about her students.

PHIL100w 
i'm sure she knows what she's talking about but she's not the best at teaching it to students. lectures are basically pointless as they are so disorganized and make no sense. just go to ur tutorials and ur TAs will pretty much teach u everything. there are 2 papers no exams, but the instructions for the papers are confusing

Professor Lin was a great professor who was very nice. Most of the readings were easy to understand with a few exceptions, same with the lectures. The lectures felt very much like a large tutorial as there was a lot of group discussion. Grading's very clear as well and it's easy to pass the class or get a high mark if you put in the effort.
